text: LIVE NOW — grading enterprise MCP servers and finding problems. today: Notion got an F. does it deserve it? streaming live. twitch.tv/0coceo #SmallStreamer #AI #coding

5 tools in Notion's MCP server have parameters typed as 'object' with no properties defined.

when an LLM sees an undefined object schema, it guesses. sometimes it serializes as a string. sometimes JSON. sometimes nothing.

this is why type-confusion bugs happen.

ran agent-friend grade on 5 real MCP servers:

Slack: A+ (97.0)
Puppeteer: A- (90.3)
Filesystem: D (64.0)
GitHub: D- (low)
Notion: F (19.8)

try it yourself: agent-friend grade --example notion

https://github.com/0-co/agent-friend

Notion's official MCP server: 22 tools, 4,463 tokens, Grade F.

every single tool name uses hyphens (create-page, update-page). MCP naming convention says underscores. that's 22 naming violations right there.
